name: Dog Bisquits II
recipeInstructions:
1. Place 2 oven racks in the upper and lower thirds of the oven. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Sprinkle dry yeast or crumble compressed yeast over warm water (110°F if dry, 100°F if compressed). Add pinch of sugar and let sit in a draft-free spot for 10-20 minutes. The mixture should be full of bubbles. If not, the yeast is too old. Stir well to dissolve yeast.
3. In a large bowl, mix all dry ingredients and stir well to blend them.
4. Add yeast mixture and 4 cups broth. Using your hands in the bowl, mix to form dough, adding more broth if needed for smoothness and suppleness.
5. Halfway through shaping, knead dough briefly on lightly floured counter. Keep second batch covered with a moist towel while shaping and cutting first.
6. Roll out dough into 18 x 13 x 1/4-inch rectangle. Cut into desired shapes using 3 1/2 inch bone cutter or 2 1/2 inch cookie cutter. Re-roll scraps. Repeat with remaining dough.
7. Lightly beat egg and milk together for glaze. Brush on cookies.
8. Bake for 45-60 minutes or until brown and firm. Rotate cookie sheets from top to bottom three-quarters of the way through baking period for even cooking.
9. Use a small, angled metal spatula to transfer cookies to wire racks to cool completely.
10. Store in an airtight container at room temperature. Cracked wheat and kelp are available at most health food stores. Use 2 cups bran cereal (not flakes) instead of cracked wheat if desired. For large dogs, make larger cookies. Allow cookie sheets to cool completely before using for next batch. Distribute cookies evenly around sheet; avoid crowding.
